State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662 322-5660 322-5901 322-6441 322-6444

September 2, 1983


Harold R. Densmore
City Attorney
City of Patterson
P.O. Box 51
Newman, CA 95360

Re: Your Request For Advice
Our File No. A-83-195

Dear Mr. Densmore:

Your letter asks questions about the possibility for
conflicts of interest arising as a result of one of the members
of the Planning Commission of the City of Patterson also serving
as a member of the Board of Directors of the Turlock Mosquito
Abatement District. Patterson lies within the area served by
the mosquito district.

Your question does not raise conflict of interest issues
under the Political Reform Act; however, you may wish to examine
a series of Attorney General's Opinions dealing with
incompatibility of office. As a starting point, I would suggest
the following:

64 Op. Atty. Gen. 795 (1981)
64 Op. Atty. Gen. 288 (1981)
64 Op. Atty. Gen. 137 (1981)
63 Op. Atty. Gen. 916 (1980)

I hope that this has been of assistance to you. If you have
any questions, you may reach me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Robert E. Leidigh
Counsel
Legal Division

REL:plh

City of Patterson
CALIFORNIA
344 West Las Palmas Avenue • P.O. Box 667 Zip 95363 • (209) 892-2041

August 24, 1983

Fair Political Practices Commission
Legal Division
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Gentlemen:

One of the members of the Planning Commission of the City of
Patterson is also serving as a member of the Board of Directors of
the Turlock Mosquito Abatement District. Members of the Planning
Commission are appointed by the Mayor with the consent of the City
Council. They receive no compensation but may be reimbursed for
expenses incurred while performing the duties of a Planning Commissioner.

The area served by the Turlock Mosquito Abatement District includes the
City of Patterson and, pursuant to the sections of the Health and
Safety Code providing for appointment of Board Members, the City member
of the Board is appointed by the City Council. The Board Members may
get a nominal compensation, I am not certain.

The function of the Planning Commission of the City of Patterson is
primarily and generally to advise and recommend to the City Council.

Under these circumstances would the person serving as a member of the
Planning Commission and a member of the Board of Trustees of the
Mosquito Abatement District have a conflict. Thank you.

Yours very truly,

[signature]
HAROLD R. DENSMORE, City Attorney
P.O. Box 51, Newman, CA 95360

HRD:cc

cc: Henry G. Hesling, City Manager

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662         322-5660            322-5901            322-6441            322-6444


August 30, 1983


Harold R. Densmore
Patterson City Attorney
P.O. Box 51
Newman, CA 95360
                                                    Re: A-83-195

Dear Mr. Densmore:

     Your letter requesting advice under the Political
Reform Act has been referred to Robert Leidighi, an attorney
in the Legal Division of the Fair Political Practices
Commission. If you have any questions about your advice
request, you may contact this attorney directly at
(916) 322-5901.

     We try to answer all advice requests promptly.
Therefore, unless your request poses particularly complex
legal questions, or unless more information is needed to
answer your request, you should expect a response within 21
working days.

                                        Very truly yours,


                                        Barbara A. Milman
                                        Chief, Legal Division

Members of the Planning Commission are appointed by the Mayor with the consent of the City Council. They receive no compensation but may be reimbursed for expenses incurred while performing the duties of a Planning Commissioner. The area served by the Turlock Mosquito Abatement District includes the City of Patterson and, pursuant to the sections of the Health and Safety Code providing for appointment Board Members, the City member of the Board is appointed by the City Council. The Board Members may get a nominal compensation, I am not certain. The function of the Planning Commission of the City of Patterson is primarily and generally to advise and recommend to the City Council. Under these circumstances would the person serving as a member of the Planning Commission and a member of the Board of Trustees of the Mosquito Abatement District have a conflict.
sections.conclusion: Your question does not raise conflict of interest issues under the Political Reform Act; however, you may wish to examine a series of Attorney General's Opinions dealing with incompatability of office. As a starting point, I would suggest the following: 64 Ope Atty. Gen. 795 (1981), 64 Ope Atty. Gen. 288 (1981), 64 Ope Atty. Gen. 137 (1981), 63 Ope Atty. Gen. 916 (1980).
sections.question_synthetic: Does a person serving simultaneously as a Planning Commission member for the City of Patterson and as a Board Director for the Turlock Mosquito Abatement District (which serves the City of Patterson) face conflict of interest issues under the Political Reform Act?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The dual service does not raise conflict of interest issues under the Political Reform Act, but the questioner should examine Attorney General's Opinions regarding incompatibility of office for potential concerns under other legal doctrines.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes both incoming request letter (dated August 24, 1983) and FPPC response letter (dated September 2, 1983). Response is from Robert E. Leidigh, Counsel, Legal Division. File reference A-83-195.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: One of the members of the Planning Commission of the City of Patterson is also serving as a member of the Board of Directors of the Turlock Mosquito Abatement District. Members of the Planning Commission are appointed by the Mayor with the consent of the City Council. They receive no compensation but may be reimbursed for expenses incurred while performing the duties of a Planning Commissioner. The area served by the Turlock Mosquito Abatement District includes the City of Patterson and, pursuant to the sections of the Health and Safety Code providing for appointment Board Members, the City member of the Board is appointed by the City Council. The Board Members may get a nominal compensation, I am not certain. The function of the Planning Commission of the City of Patterson is primarily and generally to advise and recommend to the City Council. Under these circumstances would the person serving as a member of the Planning Commission and a member of the Board of Trustees of the Mosquito Abatement District have a conflict.

CONCLUSION: Your question does not raise conflict of interest issues under the Political Reform Act; however, you may wish to examine a series of Attorney General's Opinions dealing with incompatability of office. As a starting point, I would suggest the following: 64 Ope Atty. Gen. 795 (1981), 64 Ope Atty. Gen. 288 (1981), 64 Ope Atty. Gen. 137 (1981), 63 Ope Atty. Gen. 916 (1980).

embedding.summary: FPPC advised the City Attorney of Patterson that serving simultaneously on the Planning Commission and Turlock Mosquito Abatement District Board does not create conflicts of interest under the Political Reform Act, but recommended review of incompatibility of office opinions.
